text: Unit tests are small, quick checks that verify individual parts of your code work correctly. Integration tests go a step further, confirming that different parts of your system interact smoothly. Many developers follow test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before the actual code. This approach helps prevent bugs early and ensures the design is clear. Together, these practices build confidence in your software. When a project has thorough tests, you can add new features or make changes without fear of breaking existing functionality. Testing saves time and reduces stress, especially as projects grow larger and more complex.
